---
title: "Claude Code - Converting mprcgenekeepr to Shiny Modules"
format: html
---
# Converting mprcgenekeepr to Shiny Modules

## Overview

  This guide provides a strategy for converting the mprcgenekeepr Shiny application to use Shiny modules, which will improve code organization, reusability, and maintainability.

## Benefits of Shiny Modules

1. **Namespace isolation**: Prevents ID conflicts between UI elements
2. **Code reusability**: Modules can be used multiple times in the same app
3. **Better organization**: Each feature gets its own file
4. **Easier testing**: Modules can be tested independently
5. **Team collaboration**: Different team members can work on different modules

## Recommended Module Structure

Based on the 5 main features of mprcgenekeepr, here's the proposed structure:

```
R/
â”śâ”€â”€ modQc.R                    # Quality Control module
â”śâ”€â”€ modPedigree.R              # Pedigree Creation module
â”śâ”€â”€ modPyramid.R               # Age-Sex Pyramid module
â”śâ”€â”€ modGeneticValue.R         # Genetic Value Analysis module
â”śâ”€â”€ modBreedingGroups.R       # Breeding Group Formation module
â”śâ”€â”€ modDataUpload.R           # Shared data upload functionality
â”śâ”€â”€ runApp.R                   # Main app launcher
â””â”€â”€ appUI.R                    # Main UI assembly
â””â”€â”€ appServer.R                # Main server logic
```

## Module Template

Every Shiny module consists of two functions:

```r
# UI Function - defines the user interface
modNameUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    # UI elements here, wrapping IDs with ns()
    textInput(ns("input_id"), "Label"),
    actionButton(ns("button_id"), "Click"),
    plotOutput(ns("plot_id"))
  )
}

# Server Function - defines the server logic
modNameServer <- function(id, shared_data = NULL) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Server logic here
    # Access inputs as: input$input_id (NOT ns("input_id"))

    # Return reactive values if needed by other modules
    return(
      list(
        result = reactive({ ... })
      )
    )
  })
}
```

## Example Module: Quality Control

Here's a complete example for the Quality Control module:

  ### File: `R/modQc.R`

  ```r
#' Quality Control Module - UI
#'
#' @param id Module namespace ID
#'
#' @export
modQcUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    h3("Studbook Quality Control"),

    fluidRow(
      column(
        width = 4,
        wellPanel(
          h4("Data Input"),
          fileInput(
            ns("file_input"),
            "Upload Studbook File",
            accept = c(".txt", ".csv", ".xlsx")
          ),
          numericInput(
            ns("min_parent_age"),
            "Minimum Parent Age (years)",
            value = 2,
            min = 0,
            max = 10
          ),
          checkboxInput(
            ns("use_database"),
            "Use LabKey Database",
            value = FALSE
          ),
          actionButton(
            ns("run_qc"),
            "Run Quality Control",
            class = "btn-primary"
          )
        )
      ),

      column(
        width = 8,
        tabsetPanel(
          id = ns("results_tabs"),
          tabPanel(
            "Summary",
            verbatimTextOutput(ns("qc_summary"))
          ),
          tabPanel(
            "Errors",
            DT::DTOutput(ns("qc_errors"))
          ),
          tabPanel(
            "Warnings",
            DT::DTOutput(ns("qc_warnings"))
          ),
          tabPanel(
            "Cleaned Data",
            DT::DTOutput(ns("cleaned_data"))
          )
        )
      )
    ),

    fluidRow(
      column(
        width = 12,
        downloadButton(ns("download_cleaned"), "Download Cleaned Data")
      )
    )
  )
}

#' Quality Control Module - Server
#'
#' @param id Module namespace ID
#' @param config_data Reactive containing configuration data
#'
#' @export
modQcServer <- function(id, config_data = NULL) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {

    # Reactive to store uploaded data
    uploaded_data <- reactive({
      req(input$file_input)

      file_path <- input$file_input$datapath
      file_ext <- tools::file_ext(input$file_input$name)

      if (file_ext == "xlsx") {
        data <- readxl::read_excel(file_path)
      } else {
        data <- read.csv(file_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      }

      return(data)
    })

    # Reactive to store QC results
    qc_results <- eventReactive(input$run_qc, {
      req(uploaded_data())

      withProgress(message = "Running quality control...", {

        # Call your existing QC functions
        results <- list()

        incProgress(0.2, detail = "Checking parent entries")
        # results$parent_check <- check_parents(uploaded_data())

        incProgress(0.2, detail = "Validating sex")
        # results$sex_check <- check_sex_consistency(uploaded_data())

        incProgress(0.2, detail = "Removing duplicates")
        # results$dedup <- remove_duplicates(uploaded_data())

        incProgress(0.2, detail = "Validating dates")
        # results$date_check <- validate_dates(uploaded_data())

        incProgress(0.2, detail = "Finalizing")
        # Run your actual QC pipeline here
        # results$cleaned <- qcStudbook(
        #   uploaded_data(),
        #   minParentAge = input$min_parent_age
        # )

        results
      })
    })

    # Output: Summary statistics
    output$qc_summary <- renderPrint({
      req(qc_results())

      cat("Quality Control Summary\n")
      cat("=======================\n\n")
      cat("Records processed:", nrow(uploaded_data()), "\n")
      cat("Errors found:", "X", "\n")  # Replace with actual count
      cat("Warnings:", "Y", "\n")
      cat("Records after cleaning:", "Z", "\n")
    })

    # Output: Error table
    output$qc_errors <- DT::renderDT({
      req(qc_results())
      # Return errors dataframe
      # qc_results()$errors
      data.frame(
        Row = c(1, 2),
        Error = c("Missing parent", "Invalid date"),
        Details = c("Sire ID 123 not found", "Birth date after death date")
      )
    }, options = list(pageLength = 10))

    # Output: Warnings table
    output$qc_warnings <- DT::renderDT({
      req(qc_results())
      # Return warnings dataframe
      data.frame(
        Row = c(3),
        Warning = c("Young parent"),
        Details = c("Parent age < 2 years")
      )
    }, options = list(pageLength = 10))

    # Output: Cleaned data
    output$cleaned_data <- DT::renderDT({
      req(qc_results())
      # Return cleaned data
      # qc_results()$cleaned
      uploaded_data()
    }, options = list(pageLength = 25))

    # Download handler
    output$download_cleaned <- downloadHandler(
      filename = function() {
        paste0("cleaned_studbook_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")
      },
      content = function(file) {
        # write.csv(qc_results()$cleaned, file, row.names = FALSE)
        write.csv(uploaded_data(), file, row.names = FALSE)
      }
    )

    # Return cleaned data for use by other modules
    return(
      list(
        cleaned_data = reactive({
          req(qc_results())
          # qc_results()$cleaned
          uploaded_data()
        }),
        qc_summary = reactive({
          req(qc_results())
          qc_results()
        })
      )
    )
  })
}
```

## Example Module: Age-Sex Pyramid

### File: `R/modPyramid.R`

```r
#' Age-Sex Pyramid Module - UI
#'
#' @param id Module namespace ID
#'
#' @export
modPyramidUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    h3("Age-Sex Pyramid Plot"),

    fluidRow(
      column(
        width = 3,
        wellPanel(
          h4("Plot Options"),
          selectInput(
            ns("age_unit"),
            "Age Unit",
            choices = c("Years", "Months"),
            selected = "Years"
          ),
          numericInput(
            ns("age_bin"),
            "Age Bin Size",
            value = 2,
            min = 1,
            max = 10
          ),
          checkboxInput(
            ns("show_labels"),
            "Show Count Labels",
            value = TRUE
          ),
          actionButton(
            ns("generate_plot"),
            "Generate Plot",
            class = "btn-primary"
          )
        )
      ),

      column(
        width = 9,
        plotOutput(ns("pyramid_plot"), height = "600px"),
        hr(),
        verbatimTextOutput(ns("pyramid_stats"))
      )
    )
  )
}

#' Age-Sex Pyramid Module - Server
#'
#' @param id Module namespace ID
#' @param pedigree_data Reactive containing pedigree data
#'
#' @export
modPyramidServer <- function(id, pedigree_data = NULL) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {

    # Calculate age-sex distribution
    pyramid_data <- reactive({
      req(pedigree_data())

      data <- pedigree_data()

      # Filter for living animals
      living <- data[is.na(data$exit_date) | data$exit_date == "", ]

      # Calculate ages
      # ages <- calculate_age(living$birth_date)

      # Create age bins
      # binned <- create_age_bins(ages, bin_size = input$age_bin)

      # Return processed data
      # pyramid_prep(living, age_unit = input$age_unit)
      living
    })

    # Generate plot
    output$pyramid_plot <- renderPlot({
      req(pyramid_data())
      input$generate_plot  # Trigger on button click

      isolate({
        # Call your pyramid plotting function
        # plot_age_pyramid(
        #   pyramid_data(),
        #   age_bin = input$age_bin,
        #   show_labels = input$show_labels
        # )

        # Placeholder plot
        plot(1:10, main = "Age-Sex Pyramid")
      })
    })

    # Statistics output
    output$pyramid_stats <- renderPrint({
      req(pyramid_data())

      cat("Population Statistics\n")
      cat("=====================\n\n")
      cat("Total living animals:", nrow(pyramid_data()), "\n")
      cat("Males:", "X", "\n")
      cat("Females:", "Y", "\n")
      cat("Sex ratio:", "Z", "\n")
    })

    # Return data for other modules
    return(
      list(
        pyramid_data = pyramid_data
      )
    )
  })
}
```

## Main App Structure

### File: `R/appUI.R`

```r
#' Main Application UI
#'
#' @export
appUI <- function() {
  fluidPage(
    theme = bslib::bs_theme(version = 4),

    # Application title
    titlePanel(
      div(
        img(src = "www/logo.png", height = "50px"),
        "NPRCgenekeeper - Genetic Tools for Colony Management"
      )
    ),

    # Navigation
    navbarPage(
      "",

      # Home tab
      tabPanel(
        "Home",
        h2("Welcome to NPRCgenekeeper"),
        p("Select a tool from the tabs above to get started.")
      ),

      # Quality Control tab
      tabPanel(
        "Quality Control",
        modQcUI("qcModule")
      ),

      # Pedigree Creation tab
      tabPanel(
        "Pedigree Creation",
        modPedigreeUI("pedigreeModule")
      ),

      # Age-Sex Pyramid tab
      tabPanel(
        "Age-Sex Pyramid",
        modPyramidUI("pyramidModule")
      ),

      # Genetic Value Analysis tab
      tabPanel(
        "Genetic Value Analysis",
        modGeneticValueUI("geneticValueModule")
      ),

      # Breeding Groups tab
      tabPanel(
        "Breeding Groups",
        modBreedingGroupsUI("breedingGroupsModule")
      ),

      # Settings tab
      tabPanel(
        "Settings",
        h3("Application Settings"),
        # Add settings UI here
      )
    )
  )
}
```

### File: `R/appServer.R`

```r
#' Main Application Server
#'
#' @param input Shiny input
#' @param output Shiny output
#' @param session Shiny session
#'
#' @export
appServer <- function(input, output, session) {

  # Shared reactive values
  shared_data <- reactiveValues(
    config = NULL,
    current_pedigree = NULL,
    current_studbook = NULL
  )

  # Load configuration
  observe({
    # Load config file if exists
    # shared_data$config <- load_config()
  })

  # Call modules and connect them
  qc_results <- modQcServer("qc_module")

  pedigree_results <- modPedigreeServer(
    "pedigree_module",
    cleaned_data = qc_results$cleaned_data
  )

  pyramidResults <- modPyramidServer(
    "pyramidModule",
    pedigree_data = pedigree_results$pedigree
  )

  geneticValueResults <- modGeneticValueServer(
    "genetic_value_module",
    pedigree_data = pedigree_results$pedigree
  )

  breedingGroupsResults <- modBreedingGroupsServer(
    "breedingGroupsModule",
    pedigree_data = pedigree_results$pedigree,
    genetic_values = genetic_value_results$values
  )

  # Update shared data when modules produce results
  observe({
    req(qc_results$cleaned_data())
    shared_data$current_studbook <- qc_results$cleaned_data()
  })

  observe({
    req(pedigree_results$pedigree())
    shared_data$current_pedigree <- pedigree_results$pedigree()
  })
}
```

### File: `R/run_app.R`

```r
#' Run the mprcgenekeepr Shiny Application
#'
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to shiny::runApp()
#'
#' @export
runGeneKeepR <- function(...) {

  # Add resource path for www directory
  shiny::addResourcePath(
    'www',
    system.file('www', package = 'mprcgenekeepr')
  )

  # Create and run the app
  app <- shiny::shinyApp(
    ui = appUI(),
    server = appServer
  )

  shiny::runApp(app, ...)
}
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Setup (Week 1)
1. Create module template files
2. Set up new directory structure
3. Create `appUI.R` and `appServer.R`

### Phase 2: Convert Core Modules (Weeks 2-3)
1. Start with the simplest module (Age-Sex Pyramid)
2. Convert Quality Control module
3. Test each module independently

### Phase 3: Convert Remaining Modules (Weeks 4-5)
1. Convert Pedigree Creation
2. Convert Genetic Value Analysis
3. Convert Breeding Groups

### Phase 4: Integration & Testing (Week 6)
1. Connect all modules
2. Test inter-module communication
3. Update documentation

### Phase 5: Cleanup (Week 7)
1. Remove old code
2. Update examples and vignettes
3. Final testing

## Testing Modules

Create test files in `tests/testthat/`:

  ```r
# tests/testthat/test-mod_qc.R

test_that("QC module UI creates proper structure", {
  ui <- mod_qc_ui("test")
  expect_s3_class(ui, "shiny.tag.list")
})

test_that("QC module server validates data correctly", {
  testServer(mod_qc_server, {
    # Test server logic
  })
})
```

## Best Practices

1. **Naming Convention**: Use `mod_feature_ui` and `mod_feature_server`
2. **Namespace**: Always use `ns <- NS(id)` in UI and wrap IDs with `ns()`
3. **Return Values**: Have modules return reactive values that other modules need
4. **Documentation**: Document each module with roxygen2 comments
5. **Self-Contained**: Each module should be as independent as possible
6. **Communication**: Use reactive values to pass data between modules
7. **Testing**: Write unit tests for each module

## Resources

- [Shiny Modules Documentation](https://shiny.rstudio.com/articles/modules.html)
- [Mastering Shiny - Modules Chapter](https://mastering-shiny.org/scaling-modules.html)
- [Engineering Production-Grade Shiny Apps](https://engineering-shiny.org/)
- [Golem Framework](https://thinkr-open.github.io/golem/)
